observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, prepare the
Disbursement Voucher for the unexpended current year LDRRMF for
transfer to Trust Fund before the last working day of each year and ensure
that the transfer is recorded in the books on the same year.

1.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the check for the
unexpended current year LDRRMF and deposit the same to the Special Trust
Fund bank account on or before the last working day of each year.

2.11 We recommended that the Bookkeeper and the Municipal Accountant:
a) Prepare an adjusting entry to restore the reduction of ₱ 7,033,635.00 in
the liability account - Due to LGUs - and record the actual inventory
balances in the Drugs and Medicines Inventory account and Medical,
Dental and Laboratory Supplies account as well as the corresponding
expense accounts for inventories already distributed/consumed as of
December 31, 2022;
b) Record the procured and issued medical equipment costing ₱159,975.00
in the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account.
c) Record a reduction in the Due to LGUs account and the corresponding
revenue account only when the purpose of the project/program is
completed and the Fund Utilization Report/Liquidation Reports and the
audited Report of Disbursements are duly received by the Source Agency
concerned within 60 days after its completion.

3.17 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Accountant:
a) Prepare the necessary adjusting entries on the ₱120,963.87 with unknown
details;
b) Reclassify the funds received from PCSO and LBP totalling ₱424,792.98
to the proper account codes and transfer the equivalent cash to Trust
Fund;
c) Transfer the LGSF-GEF amounting to ₱6,218,630.00 from General Fund
to Trust Fund books and prepare the necessary journal entries.

4.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the OIC-Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the
Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December
each year, an Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real
properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such
properties;
b) require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant
with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount
due and collectible for the year at the beginning of the year as basis to draw a
Journal Entry to record the RPT and SET Receivables and its
corresponding deferred accounts; and
c) direct the Municipal Accountant to review the transactions recorded
under the RPT and SET Receivables accounts and its corresponding
deferred accounts in the current and prior years to determine the cause
of the discrepancies, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
correct the affected accounts.

5.11 We recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer:
a.) furnish always the Municipal Accountant with a copy of Distribution
List of the relief goods;
b.) prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories monthly and submit
a copy thereof to the Municipal Accountant; and

5.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the relief goods
already distributed based on the Distribution List of the MDRRMO and
reconcile the ending balance based on the Report of Physical Count of
Inventories;
b.) record all welfare/relief goods for stockpiling under “Welfare Goods for
Distribution” account, and record an expense only upon issuance duly
supported with the Distribution List.

6.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a Memorandum setting the cut-off date for the submission of all money claims to the Accounting Unit, at
least five days prior to the last working day of the year.

6.7 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the proper account
titles prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs in recording
financial transactions particularly taxes withheld.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning
and Development Officer, include in the Annual Investment Plan only the
projects that are necessary and are supported with the Program of Work and
Detailed Engineering to ensure that the projects can be implemented and
completed within the budget year; and not to reserve funds for projects which
are not yet determined.

8.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MDRRM Officer
implement fully the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMF
Investment Plan in order to strengthen the LGU’s capacity to prevent and
mitigate disaster risks and respond adequately to potential damages during
disasters.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Accountant:
a) Return to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized and dormant balances
of the funds transferred from national government agencies in prior
years, regardless of the source agency;
b) Reclassify to income accounts those which are not subject to liquidation
and those amounts in which the source is unknown;
c) Maintain complete subsidiary records for each source agency and retain
copies of MOA and such other documents for every funds received from
NGAs.

10.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Head of the Human
Resource Management Office:
a) review and evaluate the staffing of each department to determine the
actual personnel needs and fill up the vacant positions in the LGU’s
plantilla in accordance with CSC rules and regulations, subject to
Personnel Services limitation;
b) limit the hiring of personnel under Job Order/Contract of Service to
those extremely necessary and deploy only to offices that actually are in
need of their services;
c) utilize the standard/ pro-forma contract of service/job orders which
contains needed attestations from the personnel to be hired under said
contracts;
d) pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by the RTWPB for
Region VIII.

1. We recommended that the Inventory Committee start the conduct of a complete physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality over a three-month period starting on July 1, 2021 or earlier, and prepare a consolidated RPCPPE, showing the complete data on PPE Type and cost, and submit the same to the Audit Team on or before November 30, 2021.
2. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer compare and reconcile the accounting and property records so that the major clean-up of all unaccounted PPE balances will be started
observations.managementAction: The designated Chairman of the Inventory Committee, issued Memorandum No. 003 series of 2021 creating an Inventory Committee. Also the Municipality’s officer-in-charge in the Assessor’s Office, Province of Leyte Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee chairman is a creating an employee who does not report to office on full capacity. Hence, she was unable to convene the committee in order to act the directives of the LCE.

observations.recommendation:
3. conduct immediately an inspection of all infrastructure assets, including road networks, and buildings owned by the Municipality; 4. assess its present value and remaining useful life; 5. prepare a report thereon with complete description of the buildings and infrastructure assets; and 6. submit the report to the Municipal Accountant before the end of CY 2021.
7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the accumulated depreciation of all depreciable properties and prepare the existing necessary accounting entries to record the depreciation expenses in order to present the correct balances in the books of accounts of the Municipality. Use the report of the Municipal Engineer in computing the depreciation of buildings and infrastructure assets.
observations.managementAction: Aside from the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer with no proper training and seminar, he is also in charge of the other programs of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Lack of knowledgeable and reliable accounting staff. Record keeping and report preparation are all done by the Municipal Accountant while existing staffs only provide assistance on routine documentation and administrative tasks.

observations.recommendation:
8. We recommended that the Supply Officer obtain the summary list of medicines dispensed to patients from the Municipal Health Unit, prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant immediately as basis of the recorded expenses on drugs and medicines.
9. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the inventory account based on the RSMI submitted by the Supply Officer to reflect the actual balance of medicines on hand.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and Management gave no explanation under the Reasons for Non-implementation column of the Status Report submitted.
observations.reason: Lack of monitoring system of management on the status of implementation for each audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee comply with the required processes for emergency procurement in the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and ensure validity and effectiveness of procurement processes.
observations.managementAction: Aside from the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer with no proper training and seminar, he is also in charge of the other programs of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Lack of familiarizing the work as a Supply Officer with no proper training and seminar, he is also in charge of the other programs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
19. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the asset inventory in observed journalizing.
20. We further recommend that the Property Officer in the Municipality prepare and submit a report on the issuances and distribution of the inventory items in order that a correct balance for both inventory asset and supplies expense may be established.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: Lack of reliable personnel with accounting. The Municipal Accountant does all the work from journalizing until the preparation of financial reports.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require LGU management to prepare a consolidated schedule of proposed trainings/seminars needs of regular employees under their supervision, in order to prevent duplication of attendance in similar trainings/seminars.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, there were no travels for trainings and seminars of LGU personnel; no consolidated schedule was prepared.

observations.recommendation: 27. We recommended that henceforth, the MDRRMO designate prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan for the Unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, with details as to the projects/programs/activities to be funded, which are related to the disaster risk reduction and management to ensure its maximum utilization.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: 28. We recommended that henceforth, management faithfully comply with the procedural and corresponding documentary requirements set forth under Item 9.1.3.4 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Report Government Transactions, regarding its repair and maintenance works on motor vehicles and equipment.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: Lack of monitoring system of management on the status of implementation for each audit recommendations. Management gave no explanation under the Reasons for Non-implementation column of the Status Report submitted.

observations.recommendation:
30. We recommend that management consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA/MOU to include the provision of target end dates/timelines upon which a particular PAP supported by a fund transfer should be implemented or completed.
31. We further recommend management designate specific personnel to be assigned to monitor project implementation and ensure its compliance with the requirement of the agreements.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: 35. The Municipal Mayor should require the accounting personnel to properly maintain and regularly reconcile their records to come up with an accurately presented balance of the PPE account and ensure fair presentation in the financial statements, as well as early detection of loss of property.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: Lack of monitoring system of management on the status of implementation for each audit recommendations. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
42. We recommend that the Accounting Section carefully and thoroughly review the financial transactions of the municipality, as well as the supporting documents attached thereto. See to it that all claims against the local government itself as well as the municipal employees/officials and suppliers involved in the transactions are supported with complete documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
43. The LCE, thru the Accounting Section, cause the immediate compliance by officials and employees concerned of the documentary requirements contained in the Notices of Suspension upon receipt thereof.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by management.
observations.reason: The incumbent Municipal Accountant asserted that a thorough review of the financial transactions could not be made as there are no available supporting documents on file which would provide information on the transaction and the municipal employees/officials and suppliers involved.

observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) issue an Office Order delegating a new Chairperson of the Inventory
Committee who reports to the Municipality on fulltime basis and can
fully discharge the duties and responsibilities as head of the said
Committee;
b) include as member of the Inventory Committee a representative from
the Accounting Unit; and
c) require the Chairman of the Inventory Committee to submit a monthly
progress report of their accomplishments to ensure that the Office
Order is being followed.

1.17 We also recommended that the Inventory Committee implement the
guidelines and procedures in inventory taking, recognition of those found at
the station, and disposition for non-existing and/ missing PPE items for one-time
cleansing of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
specifically to:
a) convene and prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the
specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date,
and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory
taking activities from start, up to the targeted completion of physical
inventory;
b) submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities;
c) conduct the inventory taking activities in accordance with the PIP,
ensuring all necessary information such as the cost and specific
identifiers like the dates and property numbers of the properties are
indicated, using the prescribed forms (Annexed in this AOM), of all
properties of the Municipality over a three-month period, starting on
July 1, 2022;
d) prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count, adopting
the uniform property identification system for PPE, showing complete
data and clustered by PPE type, and submit the same to the Audit Team
on or before December 29, 2022.

1.18 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer
compare their respective records with the latest RPCPPE and meet, at least
once a month, to identify and resolve differences between the Property Cards
(PCs) maintained by the Supply Officer and the PPELCs maintained by the
Municipal Accountant.

2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use always the proper
accounts in recording finansial transactions of the LGU based on the Revised
Chart of Accounts for LGUs prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 which
provides a brief description of Account Titles and serves as a guide in
recording and reporting the financial transactions.

3.9 We recommended that the MDRRMC, through the MDRRMO, include in the
annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate in the Municipal
Development Plan, all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to be charged
to prior years unexpended DRRMF in the Special Trust Fund.

3.10 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize, and the
Municipal Accountant process payments, for the utilization of the prior years
DRRMF in the Special Trust Fund only when the PPAs are included in the
LDRRMF Investment Plan and integrated in the AIP to ensure that the
transactions are duly authorized and to avoid disallowance in post-audit.

4.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate
subsidiary ledgers by year for transfers of the LGU’s unutilized DRRMF to
the special trust fund and record therein the related disbursements to be
charged for each year.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor execute a Memorandum of
Agreement with each recipient of financial assistance stipulating therein the
reciprocal duties and responsibilities for the use of the funds and its
implementation including the reportorial requirements to be complied and
submitted to the municipality.

5.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) process the release of fund transfers only when supported with
complete documentary requirements from the recipients which
includes the submission of a Memorandum of Agreement; and
b) issue Demand Letters, for signature of the Municipal Mayor, to the
eight (8) recipient-barangays for submission of the Report of Checks
Issued (RCI) and the Report of Disbursement (RD) on the utilization of
the funds they received amounting to P25,000.00 each.

5.11 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor grant financial assistance
funded from the 20% Development Fund only if the amount will be utilized by
the recipients for development projects.

6.11 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Audit Team the
lacking documents mentioned in paragraph 1.9 to validate the eligibility and
entitlement of the claimants who received the SRA and AHDP totaling
₱777,499.96.

6.12 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the
books of the LGU the Liquidation Reports on cash advances only if these are
supported with pertinent necessary documents.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor sign a Memorandum of
Agreement with any government agency when the necessary information are
completely incorporated therein, specifically the duration of the
implementation of the program to set the parameters in the program
implementation.

7.10 We also recommended that:
a) the Special Disbursing Officer coordinate with the Program
Coordinator on the submission to the Audit Team of the lacking
documents mentioned in paragraph 1.6 to validate the
implementation of the program;
b) The Program Coordinator:
i. conduct thorough profiling of the intended beneficiaries in future
programs of the LGU and indicate specific information as
instructed in the forms to be accomplished;
ii. mark completely the necessary sections in the Time Book and
Payroll before submitting the report to the source agency;
c) The Municipal Accountant, upon compliance with the lacking
documents, prepare and submit immediately the FUR to DOLE, copy
furnished the Audit Team.

8.10 We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members and Municipal
Budget Officer limit the appropriation for extraordinary and miscellaneous
expenses within the amount prescribed in Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160 in
order to prevent the incurrence of obligations without legal basis.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all
Department Heads to indicate in their respective Purchase Requests the
complete details and specifications of the items being requisitioned as guide in
obtaining price quotations.

9.9 We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee indicate in all
Purchase Orders the definite terms, conditions and basic information of the
procurement being made as required in COA Circular No. 96-010.

10.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant follow the detailed
Instructions on the preparation of accounting books, records, forms, and
reports to adequately supply the needed information, and specifically to:
a) Assign control numbers on the DVs and JEVs and provide brief
explanation of each transaction on the face of the JEVs to distinguish
one document from the other for easy referencing and monitoring of
accounting records and transaction documents;
b) Provide the DV and JEV numbers in all the accounting Journals
(Check Disbursements, Cash Disbursement, Cash Receipts, and
General Journal); and
c) List the reference/supporting documents on the face of the JEVs and
the DVs.

11. We recommended that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat indicate always in the Invitation to Bid (IB) the correct information of the items for bidding, post the correct IB in the PhilGEPS website, and review carefully the eligibility documents of prospective bidders to truly promote competition in the best interest of the Municipal Government of Tabontabon.
observations.managementAction: Practiced due diligence in awarding contracts

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee exercise prudence and refrain from transacting with third-party suppliers in order to ensure that procurement transactions would not be prejudicial to the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Payment of regular operating expenses out of the disaster risk fund was stopped.

observations.recommendation: 13. We recommended that the BAC award contracts only to suppliers and contractors who are legally, technically and financially capable to undertake the contracts to be procured, based on the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR to ensure that the best price advantageous to the government will be obtained.
observations.managementAction: Practice diligence in awarding contracts

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize the utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster risk reduction and discontinue the use of the regular LDRRMF for the payment of regular operating expenses of the General Fund, to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management included in the MDRRM Investment Plan projects and activities which are for disaster risk reduction only and provide a budget for repair of the rescue vehicle in the General Fund when appropriating for the MDRRM Office.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the correct withholding taxes from all government money payments to suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
17. We recommended that the MSWDO evaluate thoroughly the claims for burial assistance to ensure proper entitlement by targeted beneficiaries.
18. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements for burial assistance are supported by the certificate of indigency issued by the MSWDO.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommend that the Chairperson of the GFPS TWG be required to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2019. Henceforth, the same officer should be required to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA audit team.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2020 was submitted January 16, 2020. The GAD Chairperson gave no explanation under the Reasons for Non-implementation column of the Status Report submitted.
observations.reason: Lack of monitoring system of management on the status of implementation for each audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
23. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the remaining variance of P108,000.00 from the General Fund to the LDRRM Trust Fund.
24. We likewise recommend that the MDRRMO designate coordinate with concerned department heads on the utilization of the LDRRMF and ensure the correctness of the computation of the amount of unexpended balance to be transferred to Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit an explanation on the lack of information in the procurement documents.
26. We further recommend that management submit a status report on the utility and performance of these solar panels.
observations.managementAction: Written explanation on the lack of information in the procurement documents was submitted.
observations.reason: Management gave no explanation under the Reasons for Non-implementation column of the Status Report submitted.

observations.recommendation: 29. We recommended that management strictly observe prudence on the use of allocated funds for food expenses and consider a clear prioritization of the nature and extent of the transactions that may be charged to it.
observations.managementAction: Management no longer charged food expenses for regular department meetings.

observations.recommendation: 32. Demand letters were immediately issued to concerned officers and employees for the liquidation of cash advances. 33. Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents by granting of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already issued to the accountable officers. As of December 31, 2021, the balance of officers and employees cash advances remained for cash advances includes cash advances aging over 3 years.
observations.reason: Demand letters were already issued. The balance still includes an undetermined cash advances of P1,018,907.72.

observations.recommendation:
36. Management should take immediate steps to enforce titling of the real properties owned by the Municipality which are currently covered only by tax declarations.
37. The Municipal Accountant and Assessor should reconcile their records and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: This matter was already presented to the LCE for discussion.

observations.recommendation:
39. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to strictly adhere to the basic principle of paying claims in a form of check.
40. Limit the granting of cash advance for field operation/activity only or in some activities where it is impractical to pay by check.
41. The Municipal Accountant should demand the immediate liquidation of his outstanding cash advances and enforce the withholding of his salary and allowances in settlement thereof.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Demand letters were issued. Balance of former Municipal Treasurer Erik Aguilar amounting to P72,200.00 still remains unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that the Inventory Committee start the conduct of a
complete physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality over a
three-month period starting on July 1, 2021 or earlier, and prepare a
consolidated RPCPPE, showing the complete data on PPE Type and cost,
and submit the same to the Audit Team on or before November 30, 2021.
1.9 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Supply
Officer compare and reconcile the accounting and property records so
that the major clean-up of all unaccounted PPE balances will be
started.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
a) conduct immediately an inspection of all infrastructure assets,
including road networks, and buildings owned by the Municipality;
b) assess it present value and remaining useful life;
c) prepare a report thereon with complete description of the buildings
and infrastructure assets; and
d) submit the report to the Municipal Accountant before the end of CY
2021.
2.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the
accumulated depreciation of all depreciable properties and prepare the
necessary accounting entries to record the depreciation expenses in order
to present the correct balances in the books of accounts of the
Municipality. Use the report of the Municipal Engineer in computing the
depreciation of buildings and infrastructure assets.

3.13 We recommended that the Supply Officer obtain the summary list of
medicines dispensed to patients from the Municipal Health Unit,
prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and submit
the same to the Municipal Accountant immediately as basis of the
recorded expenses on drugs and medicines.
3.14 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the
inventory account based on the RSMI submitted by the Supply Officer to
reflect the actual balance of medicines on hand.

4.8 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee practice
continuous posting of the NOA/contract/Purchase Order of awarded
projects in the GPPB online portal for emergency procurement, in
connection with the on-going requirements prescribed by the Bayanihan Act
for reporting and monitoring purposes of fund utilization, in relation to the
existing CoViD19 pandemic.

5.12 We recommended that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee
and its Secretariat indicate always in the Invitation to Bid (IB) the correct
information of the items for bidding, post the correct IB in the PhilGEPS
website, and review carefully the eligibility documents of prospective
bidders to truly promote competition in the best interest of the Municipal
Government of Tabontabon.

6.6 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee exercise
prudence and refrain from transacting with third-party suppliers in
order to ensure that procurement transactions would not be prejudicial to
the interest of the municipality.
6.7 This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance, if warranted.

7.10 We recommended that the BAC award contracts only to suppliers and
contractors who are legally, technically and financially capable to
undertake the contracts to be procured, based on the provisions of RA
No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR to ensure that the best price
advantageous to the government will be obtained.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize the utilization of
the LDRRMF for disaster risk reduction and management activities only and
discontinue the use of LDRRMF for the payment of regular operating
expenses to avoid audit disallowances.
8.11 We also recommended that the MDRRM Officer include in the
succeeding year’s LDRRMF Investment Plan projects and activities
which are for disaster risk reduction and management activities only and
provide a budget for repair of the rescue vehicle in the General Fund
when appropriating for the MDRRM Office.

9.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the correct
withholding taxes from all government money payments to suppliers.

10.12 We recommended that the MSWDO evaluate thoroughly the claims for
burial assistance to ensure proper entitlement by targeted beneficiaries.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that
disbursements for burial assistance are supported by the certificate of
indigency issued by the MSWDO.

We recommended that the MDRRMO Designate prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan for the Unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, with details as to the projects/programs/activities to be funded, which are related to the disaster risk reduction and management to ensure its maximum utilization.
observations.reason: No LDRRMF Investment Plan for the Unexpended LDRRMF of previous years was prepared during CY 2019 due to the con-current positions being held by the MDRRMO Designate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, management faithfully comply with the procedural and corresponding documentary requirements set forth under Item 9.1.3.4 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions, regarding its repair and maintenance works on motor vehicles and equipment.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers during CY 2019 pertaining to these transactions still did not include documents showing that the pre-requisite evaluation and/or inspection procedures were conducted.

observations.recommendation: The Accounting Section should carefully and thoroughly review the financial transactions of the municipality, as well as the supporting documents attached thereto. See to it that all claims against the local government unit are supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445. The LCE, thru the Accounting Section, cause the immediate compliance by officials and employees concerned of the documentary requirements contained in the Notices of Suspension upon receipt thereof.
observations.managementAction: The incumbent Municipal Accountant asserted that a thorough review of the financial transactions could not be made as there are no available supporting documents on file which would provide information on the transaction itself as well as the municipal employees/officials and suppliers involved in the transactions.
observations.reason: The incumbent Municipal Accountant asserted that a thorough review of the financial transactions could not be made as there are no available supporting documents on file which would provide information on the transaction itself as well as the municipal employees/officials and suppliers involved in the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the asset inventory in above-observed transactions.  We further recommended that the Property Officer in the Municipality  prepare  and  submit  a  report  on  the  issuances  and  distribution of the inventory items in order that  a  correct  balance for both inventory asset and supplies expense may be established.
We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  draw  a  Journal  Entry Voucher to adjust the income accounts used for the above transaction.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record mobilization  fees in its proper account title as Advances to Contractors pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for the Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Chairperson of  the  GFPS  TWG  be  required  to submit GAD Accomplishment Report for CY  2019.  Henceforth,  the  same officer should be required  to  strictly  adhere  to  Item  V  of  COA  Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA audit team.

We recommended that the LGU Department Heads be required to prepare  a consolidated schedule of proposed trainings/seminars needs of  regular employees under their supervision, in order to prevent duplication  of attendance in similar trainings/seminars.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to immediately transfer the remaining variance of approximately P108,000.00 from  the General Fund to the LDRRM Trust Fund. We likewise recommended that the MDRRMO-designate coordinate  with concerned  department  heads  on  the utilization  of  the  LDRRMF  and  ensure  the  correctness   of   the computation of the amount of unexpended balance to be  transferred  to Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget  Officer  and  the  Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, be directed to strictly monitor  both  the  unutilized  balances  of  funds  and  the availability of cash balances especially those intended for continuing appropriations in order to avoid future balance deficits.


    We recommended further that management  refrain  from  using  unexpended balances of funds unless its full availability can be established.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee  submit  explanation  on the lack of information in the procurement documents.   We  further recommended that management submit a status report on the utility  and performance of these procured solar panels.
We recommended that management cease the practice of charging  regular operating expenses against its Trust Fund, pursuant to Section  4  (3)  of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record retention money transactions against completed infrastructure projects in its proper account designation as Guarantee/Security Deposits Payable pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The recording of retention money in its proper account has been implemented and erroneous recording to Bail Bonds Payable has been discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, advise the municipality’s JO personnel on the requirements and procedure of remitting their personal shares to the HDMF as self-employed individuals, in order that such activity may be discontinued from the municipality’s official functions.
observations.managementAction: This practice of making deductions from the Job Order payrolls and facilitating the remittance of employees’ share has already been stopped from the municipality’s official functions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strengthen its post-qualification process for winning bidders handling multiple contracts, by requiring commitment not only on the quantitative aspect, but also on the qualitative performance on the handled projects, pursuant to Section 34.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Post-qualification activity is conducted by members of the BAC to ensure that the bidder declared the LCRB can implement and perform completely and effectively on the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the strict implementation of the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 on the allocation and utilization of LDRRMF. Specifically, the LGU should stop the payment of regular operating expenses using the amount appropriated for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Payment of regular operating expenses charged to the amount appropriated for LDRRM projects and programs has been discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require suppliers to issue a Sales Invoice or Official Receipt, whenever applicable, every time payment is made to the supplier/seller.
observations.managementAction: From CY 2019 onwards, Sales Invoice or Official Receipt was always required by the LGU from the suppliers whenever payment is made for every transactio ns with the suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the Contracts of Service or Memorandum of Agreement between the municipality and the worker shall properly state or establish the latter’s specific duties and responsibilities, taking into account the conditions discouraged by CSC Memorandum Circular No.17, series of 2002.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already enforced the implementation of the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 17, series of 2002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe prudence on the use of the allocated funds for representation expenses, and consider a clear prioritization of the nature and extent of the transactions that may be charged to it.
observations.managementAction: Management no longer charged food expenses during regular department heads’ meetings.
observations.reason: The nature of transactions charged against representation expenses, particularly the meals and snacks served during meetings of various committees, could not be verified due to non-submission of documents that shows the meeting agenda and its minutes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make representation with grantor NGAs and consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA/MOU to include the provision of target dates/timelines upon which a particular PAP supported by a fund transfer should be implemented or completed. We further recommend management designate specific personnel to be assigned to monitor project implementation and ensure its compliance with the requirement of the agreements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had come up with an Omnibus Project Monitoring Team tasked to monitor and evaluate the implementati on of the municipal-wide projects. The management would strengthen the capacities of the said team to fully address their functions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management judiciously undertake the procurement process to ensure effective competition in the selection of winning bidders and suppliers and to monitor and sanction accountability for breakdown in controls established under RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Whenever a program, project, activity and consulting services is scheduled for implementa- tion at the LGU, the BAC had undergone the protocol of the bidding process as prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA 9184, thus, a legitimate bidder is declared to be having the Lowest Calculated Responsive Bid (LCRB).

observations.recommendation: a) Demand immediate liquidation of cash advances by preparing demand letters to be issued to concerned officers and employees for cash advances that remained unliquidated/ unsettled for more than 30 days. b) Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents by not granting of cash advance to those with unliquidated balances. c) Undertake, as a last resort, appropriate legal actions for the recovery and/or settlement of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: a) Demand letters were already issued to the concerned officers and employees for cash advances that remained unliquidated/unsettled for more than 30 days. b) No additional cash advances were granted to officers and employees with unliquidated balances. c) Appropriate legal actions for the recovery and/or settlement of unliquidated balances were undertaken as necessary.
observations.reason: Demand letters were already issued to those accountable.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer should prepare the necessary plans and schedules to concretize and ensure the timely implementation of the projects for an efficient, effective and economical utilization of government funds and achievement of the project objectives within the expected timeframe for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Fund transfers from various NGA’s aggregating P5,444,613.00 for the purchase of equipment and construction of property had already been fully implemented and 100 percent completed.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should enjoin all department heads involved in the development projects to monitor its implementation and to ensure that funds allocated for the 20% EDF be optimally utilized during the year in accordance with the time frame of project implementation that should be properly indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive advised all department heads in the development projects to make a tracking table to easily and closely monitor the implementati on of all PPA’s. Those not fully utilized during the year were reprogrammed through the EMDC. The LGU has enforced the implementati on of prioritized PPA’s to fully utilize the funds.
observations.reason: The Local Chief Executive advised all department heads in the development projects to make a tracking table to easily and closely monitor the implementati on of all PPA’s. Those not fully utilized during the year were reprogrammed through the EMDC. The LGU has enforced the implementati on of prioritized PPA’s to fully utilize the funds.

observations.recommendation: The GAD Focal Person should plan, focus and undertake definite activities such as: a) Enhancement of women’s access to all types of health care; b) Lowering of maternal mortality rates and provide adequate family planning services; c) Improvement of the situation of women in the informal economy; d) Creating awareness for women who are victims of violence to report their ordeal, to proper authorities.
observations.managementAction: The following activities were already undertaken by the GAD Focal Person: Conducted information and dissemination campaign with regards to women’s access to all types of health care and awareness on violence against women. Conducted health teaching during prenatal and family day and strict compliance to the home delivery ordinance.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor should require the Accounting and Property personnel to properly maintain and regularly reconcile their records to come up with an accurately presented balance of the PPE account and ensure fair presentation in the FS, as well as early detection of loss of property.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE as of December 31, 2018 was submitted by the LGU, but we observed, upon validation, that several items per books were not included in the items present in the said report. No same report was submitted as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: RPCPPE as of December 31, 2018 was submitted by the LGU, but we observed, upon validation, that several items per books were not included in the items present in the said report. No same report was submitted as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: Management should prioritize and consider filling up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the LGU for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: LGU had taken cognizance and prioritized the filling up of vacant positions for efficient delivery of services. Appointments for Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, and Midwife were already approved by the CSC. While the processing of appointments of other department heads like MDRRMO and Municipal Engineer are already in progress or in the pipeline.

observations.recommendation: 1) Management should take immediate steps to enforce titling of the real properties owned by the Municipality which are currently covered only by tax declarations. 2) The Municipal Accountant and Assessor should reconcile their records and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: This matter was already presented to the LCE for discussion. Reconciliation and necessary adjustments will be made as soon as the titling of the properties will be fully completed.

observations.recommendation: 1) The Municipal Mayor should require the concerned officials to strictly adhere to the basic principle of paying claims in a form of check. 2) Limit the granting of cash advance for field operation/ activity only or in some activities where it is impractical to pay by check. 3) The Municipal Accountant should demand the Municipal Treasurer for the immediate liquidation of his outstanding cash advances and enforce the withholding of his salary and allowances in settlement thereof.
observations.managementAction: Despite the issuance of demand letters and non-granting of additional cash advance to those with unliquidated balance, the amount of total cash advance remains material. Demand letter for the immediate liquidation of cash advances totalling P302,900.00 attributed to Former Municipal Treasurer Erik Aguilar was already issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Section carefully and thoroughly review the financial transactions of the municipality, as well as the supporting documents attached thereto. That all claims against the local government unit are supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445. The LCE, thru the Accounting Section, cause the immediate compliance of officials and employees concerned of the documentary requirements contained in the Notices of Suspension upon receipt thereof.
observations.managementAction: The incumbent Municipal Accountant asserted that a thorough review of the financial transactions could not be made as there are no available supporting documents on file which would cause information on the transaction itself as well as the municipal employees/officials and suppliers involved in the transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
Municipal Treasurer, exert effort in determining the details of the reconciling
items per books and to consider making the necessary adjustment to add the
stale checks balance back to the account Cash in Bank pursuant to
Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record retention
money transactions against completed infrastructure projects in its proper
account designation as Guarantee/Security Deposits Payable pursuant to the
Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination
with the Municipal Treasurer, advise the municipalitys JO personnel on the
requirements and procedure of remitting their personal shares to the HDMF
as self-employed individuals, in order that such activity may be discontinued
from the municipalitys official functions.

We recommended that management strengthen its post-qualification
process for winning bidders handling multiple contracts, by requiring
commitment not only on the quantitative aspect, but also on the qualitative
performance on the handled projects, pursuant to Section 34.3 of the Revised
IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the MDRRMO Designate prepare annually the
LDRRMF Investment Plan for the Unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, with
details as to the projects/programs/activities to be funded, which are related to the
disaster risk reduction and management to ensure its maximum utilization.

We recommended for the strict implementation of the provisions of
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 on the
allocation and utilization of LDRRMF. Specifically, the LGU should stop the
payment of regular operating expenses using the amount appropriated for LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require suppliers to
issue a Sales Invoice or Official Receipt, whenever applicable, every time
payment is made to the supplier/seller.

We recommended that henceforth, the Contracts of Service or Memorandum
of Agreement between the municipality and the worker shall properly state or
establish the latters specific duties and responsibilities, taking into account the
conditions discouraged by CSC Memorandum Circular No.17, series of 2002,
which circularized CSC Resolution No. 020790.

We recommended that henceforth, management faithfully comply with the
procedural and corresponding documentary requirements set forth under Item
9.1.3.4 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government
Transactions, regarding its repair and maintenance works on motor vehicles and
equipment.

We recommended that management strictly observe prudence on the use of the
allocated funds for representation expenses, and consider a clear prioritization
of the nature and extent of the transactions that may be charged to it.

We recommend that management implement strictly the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 2, 2017 on the utilization of the 20% of Internal Revenue Allotment for development projects. Specifically, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant are advised to avoid using this fund to pay for regular operating expenses. We likewise reiterate our recommendation on the optimal utilization of this fund for the development of the LGU’s socio- economic condition which will benefit its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant are advised to avoid using this fund to pay for regular operating expenses. We likewise reiterate our recommendation on the optimal utilization of this fund for the development of the LGU’s socio- economic condition which will benefit its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board be directed to re-evaluate its School Board Budget to avoid inappropriate charges to SEF and to strictly observe the prioritization of expenses chargeable to SEF as provided for in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017.

We recommend that management make representation with grantor NGAs and consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA/MOU to include the provision of target end dates upon which a particular PAP supported by a fund transfer should be implemented or completed. We further recommend that management designate specific personnel to be assigned to monitor project implementation and ensure its compliance with the requirement of the agreements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had implemented Omnibus Project Monitoring Team tasked to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the municipal-wide projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management judiciously undertake the procurement process to ensure effective competition in the selection of winning bidders and suppliers and to monitor and sanction accountability for breakdown in controls established under RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Upon validation, we still noted lapses in the post-qualification procedures performed by the LGU’s Bids and Awards Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management revisit the guidelines on travel and attendance in trainings/seminars pursuant to EO No. 298 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2010-139 dated December 2, 2010, in order to ensure that traveling activities are done with an eye on the prudent use of scarce financial resources versus the achievement of desired organizational results and development outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the rules and regulations and the submission of all necessary documents required in EO 298 for travel claims. Further, we likewise recommend to require all employees and officials to use electronic copy of official receipts instead of an RER for claims of taxi fare.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” not only the disbursement vouchers but also its supporting documents after payment to prevent any attempt to re-use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of original documents to be attached to the DVs before it will be processed for payment.

We recommend a) Demand immediate liquidation of advances by preparing demand letters to be issued to concerned officers and employees for cash advances that remained unliquidated/ unsettled for more than 30 days. b) Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents by not granting of cash advance to those with cash unliquidated balances. c) Undertake, as a last resort, appropriate legal actions for the recovery and/or settlement of unliquidated cash balances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters to concerned officers and employees for cash advances that remained unliquidated/ unsettled for more than 30 days were already issued to those concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Planning and Development Officer should prepare the necessary plans and schedules to concretize implementation for an efficient, effective and economical utilization of government funds and achievement of the project objectives within the expected timeframe for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Necessary plans and scheduling to ensure implementation were already prepared by the Municipal Planning and Development Officer.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should enjoin all department heads involved in the development projects to monitor its implementation and to ensure that funds allocated for the 20% EDF be optimally utilized during the year in accordance with the time frame of project implementation that should be properly indicated in the Annual Investment Plan. Those not fully utilized during the year were reprogrammed through the EMDC.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has enforced the implementation of prioritized PPA’s to fully utilize the funds.

observations.recommendation: The GAD Focal Person should plan, focus and undertake definite activities such as: a) Enhancement of women’s access to all types of health care; b) Lowering of maternal mortality rates and provide adequate family planning services; c) Improvement of the situation of women in the informal economy; d) Creating awareness for women who are victims of violence to report their ordeal, to proper authorities.
observations.managementAction: GAD activities were undertaken during the year.

observations.recommendation: The management should formulate marketing plans and strategies to encourage lessees/ vendors to utilize the new public market building in order to generate income that could be used for its operations and do not allow vendors to operate outside of the vicinity of the public market.

We recommend: The Municipal Mayor should require the Accounting and Property personnel to properly maintain and regularly reconcile their records to come up with an accurately presented balance of the PPE account and ensure fair presentation in the FS, as well as early detection of loss of property.
observations.managementAction: An RPCPPE was submitted by the LGU, but we observed, upon validation, that several items per accurately books were not included in the said report.

observations.recommendation: Management should prioritize and consider filling up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the LGU for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: LGU had taken cognizance and prioritized the filling up of vacant positions. The processing of the appointments for Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, and Midwife are already in progress or in the pipeline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend: 1) Management should take immediate steps to enforce titling of the real properties owned by the Municipality which are currently covered only by tax declarations. 2) The Municipal Accountant and Assessor should reconcile their records and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: This matter was already presented to the LCE for discussion.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor should direct the officials concerned to: 1) design a strategic plan to boost revenue collection efforts with calculated quarterly payment/ installments and periodic monitoring to check ineffective strategies and weakness in the system; 2) undertake integrated/ mass revenue collection campaign and information drive to increase tax consciousness among taxpayers, particularly on the programs/ projects that could be possible funded therefrom; and 3) exercise all administrative and judicial remedies provided by law to collect the unpaid property taxes.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. A strategic plan to boost revenue collection and an information drive to increase tax consciousness were already undertaken by the Municipal Treasurer. Demand letters were also issued to delinquent taxpayers and furnished to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to facilitate auction sale of properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend: 1) The Municipal Mayor should require the concerned officials to strictly adhere to the basic principle of paying claims in a form of check. 2) Limit the granting of cash advance for field operation/ activity only or in some activities where it is impractical to pay by check. 3) The Municipal Accountant should demand the Municipal Treasurer for immediate liquidation of his outstanding cash advances and enforce the withholding of his salary and allowances in settlement thereof.
observations.managementAction: Despite the issuance of demand letters and non-granting of additional cash advance to officers with unliquidated balance, the amount of total cash advance remains material.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant should submit accounts and reports for the said period immediately. The Municipal Mayor should enjoin the concerned officials to submit the accounts and financial reports of the municipality for the ensuing year in accordance with aforementioned guidelines. Otherwise, if the concerned officials still fail to comply with the audit recommendation, they should be filed with administrative disciplinary charges as prescribed in Section 127 of PD 1445 and Section 26(3), Book V of EO 292.
observations.managementAction: The deadline was complied with and the financial statements and supporting schedules have already been submitted within deadline.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned should furnish the auditor with copies of notice of delivery and request for inspection at the appropriate time as prescribed under COA Circular 95-006. The Accountant should record purchases of supplies and materials as inventory following the moving average method. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant should maintain property cards/ stock cards and ledger cards, respectively, for each category of asset/inventories and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) as basis in the recording of expense in the books of accounts for the issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Notices of delivery and inspection reports on purchases are already attached to submitted disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management implement strictly the provisions of
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 2, 2017 on
the utilization of the 20% of Internal Revenue Allotment for development
projects. Specifically, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
are advised to avoid using this fund to pay for regular operating expenses.

We likewise reiterate our previous recommendation on the optimal
utilization of this fund for the development of the LGU’s socio- economic
condition which will benefit its constituents.

We recommend that the Local School Board be directed to re-evaluate its
School Board Budget to avoid inappropriate charges to SEF and to strictly
observe the prioritization of expenses chargeable to SEF as provided for in
Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of
2017.

We recommend that management make representation with grantor
NGAs and consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA/MOU to
include the provision of target dates/timelines upon which a particular PAP
supported by a fund transfer should be implemented or completed.

We further recommend management designate specific personnel to be
assigned to monitor project implementation and ensure its compliance with the
requirement of the agreements.

We recommend that management judiciously undertake the procurement
process to ensure effective competition in the selection of winning bidders and
suppliers and to monitor and sanction accountability for breakdown in controls
established under RA 9184.

We recommend that management revisit the guidelines on travel and
attendance in trainings/seminars pursuant to EO No. 298 and DILG
Memorandum Circular 2010-139 dated December 2, 2010, in order to ensure
that traveling activities are done with an eye on the prudent use of scarce
financial resources versus the achievement of desired organizational results and
development outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the rules
and regulations and the submission of all necessary documents required in
EO 298 for travel claims. Further, we likewise recommend to require all
employees and officials to use electronic copy of official receipts instead of an
RER for claims of taxi fare.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” not only the
disbursement vouchers but also its supporting documents after payment to
prevent any attempt to re-use the same supporting documents in payment of
future claims. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant require
the submission of original documents to be attached to the DVs before it will be
processed for payment.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management:
a) Demand immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances by preparing demands
letters to be issued to concerned officers and employees for cash advances that
remained unliquidated/unsettled for more than 30 days.
c) Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely
submission of liquidation documents by not granting of cash advance to those with
unliquidated balances.
d) Undertake, as a last resort, appropriate legal actions for the recovery and/or
settlement of unliquidated cash advances.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.

We recommend that Management through the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer, prepare the necessary plans and schedules to concretize and
ensure the timely implementation of the projects for an efficient, effective and
economical utilization of government funds and achievement of the project objectives
within the expected timeframe for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer strictly follow Section 69 No. 4 of
Presidential Decree No 1445 and DBM-DOF Joint Circular No. 1-81 dated January
1, 1981. Strengthen additional control measures on cash management to eliminate
opportunities of retaining collections in the hands of the Revenue Collecting Clerk
(RCC).
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin all department heads
involved in the development projects to monitor its implementation and to ensure that
funds allocated for the 20% EDF be optimally utilized during the year in accordance
with the time frame of project implementation that should be properly indicated in
the Annual Investment Plan.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow Implementing
Guidelines 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 by adjusting
the books through the following adjusting journal entry:
Due from LGUs xx
Subsidy to LGUs xx
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.

We recommend that the Acting Property Officer prepare and use the proper format of
the IIRUP for the unserviceable property and facilitate its proper disposal in
accordance with prescribed rules and regulations.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer conduct an inventory of the LGU’s road
network system and the Accountant to subsequently record in tis books the amount of
the road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage of 25%
for the calendar year 2016.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.

We recommend that management formulate marketing plans and strategies to
encourage lessees/vendors to utilize the new public market building in order to
generate income that could be used for its operations and do not allow vendors to
operate outside of the vicinity of the public market.
Schedule a “tabo” or market day when most of the farmers can sell/display their
produce and require the activity to be held in the new Public Market.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of
Disallowance.
